body.title: [triage] Consequence check: recall(X) API and memory schema v2
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate.

**AND NOTE WHO FAILED HERE.** Akien wrote none of these tickets. **CC wrote all 149.** So 'the design step that failed' is CC, and the honest rule is not 'a human must declare it' — it is: **THE AUTHOR DECLARES THE INTENTION AT AUTHORING TIME, FROM THE CONVERSATION THAT PRODUCED THE TICKET.** That is what makes it `declared`: not that a human typed it (a ratified string and a machine-written string are byte-identical), but that it was stated from evidence INDEPENDENT of the artifact — the design conversation, which traces to Akien's intent. An intention RECONSTRUCTED LATER FROM THE TICKET BODY has no independent evidence and is `extracted-from-artifact`: a description, never a spec (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13). **The failure was not forgetting a field. It was filing 149 tickets without ever pausing to say what I intended by them.**

---

**What changed:** Added recall(X) API with inference-free retrieval (FTS + pre-computed embeddings + typed graph) and memory schema v2 (payloads jsonb, edge_type, source_agent).
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) Scraps embedding adds write-time latency — Igor's memory deposit path may slow noticeably; watch for slowdowns. (2) edge_type constraint may silently reject edge writes from callers not yet updated — log for failures. (3) recall(X) vs research(X) split may cause agent confusion — agents may call research when recall suffices, wasting inference. (4) Pre-computed embeddings have model dependency — if model changes, old vectors silently degrade in similarity quality.
**Signals to watch:** memory write latency in logs; edge write error counts; inference call count per recall in Librarian logs; embedding model metadata consistency across clan.memories rows.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-11.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete
